nodes.title: IS:HCOL Thesis Prep
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: App to Hlth: From Pers to Syst
nodes.description: This course introduces students to a range of topics related to their chosen majors and future careers. Pre/co-requisite: First year College of Nursing and Health Sciences students.
nodes.other_requirements: First year College of Nursing and Health Sciences students. First time first year CNHS Majors: CSD, EXSC, MLS, MRS, PHS only. PRNU Majors must sign up for Section B; Also additional meeting times: Wednesdays, Sept 9, Oct 7, and Nov 4; Synchronous online meetings;

nodes.title: HC: Honors Project and Seminar
nodes.description: This course facilitates the Honors College project. All CNHS Honors College students must enroll.

nodes.title: ST: Aging Well, Med Style
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; Program Fee:  $$2,029 minus $450 deposit; OIE GoAbroad App Deadline: 11/18/2025; Out of Pocket expenses (approx. $190) are additional; Airfare is additional; Pre/Post departure meetings 1/27, 2/24 & 4/14/26

nodes.title: Health Care Ethics
nodes.description: A study of ethical principles and applications used to help resolve dilemmas in health care delivery. Introduction to ethical decision-making models used in the practice of modern health care.

nodes.title: NH Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; Days/times TBD. Contact instructor for day, time and location

nodes.title: Honors College Thesis Preparat
nodes.description: Supports College of Nursing and Health Sciences' Honors College students begin planning their thesis and developing a research literature review on a specific thesis topic. Prerequisites: Junior standing, College of Nursing and Health Sciences' Honors College student.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior standing, College of Nursing and Health Sciences' Honors College student. CNHS HCOL Students Only; variable credits 1-3

nodes.title: Fundamentals Critical Inquiry
nodes.description: Interprofessional research/evidence-based practice course; instructs students from a variety of healthcare professions to develop skills in research/clinical question development, appraisal of literature and practice guidelines, experimental, quantitative and qualitative studies, and a fundamental understanding of evidence to inform their practice.

nodes.title: Int: NH Health Service Intern
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
